stimulus: The lifespans of tortoises vary dramatically by species. For example, the Egyptian tortoise (Testudo kleinmanni) typically lives for only a few ______blank the Aldabra giant tortoise (Aldabrachelys gigantea) can reach an age of more than 150 years.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is the use of punctuation between an independent clause and a subordinate clause introduced by "while." A comma is conventionally used to mark this kind of boundary when the subordinate clause follows the main clause and presents a contrast.

Choice A is incorrect because a colon can't be used to introduce a subordinate clause in this way. Choice B is incorrect because a semicolon would suggest two independent clauses, but "while..." is a dependent clause. Choice D is incorrect because, although it is grammatically possible to end the first clause with a period and start a new sentence, the choice as written keeps the subordinator "While," which would awkwardly begin a new sentence treating a dependent clause as if it were independent.
